segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a screen recording of the 'Threaducation Patreon Page', featuring its yellow and black design and displaying membership tiers like 'The Rick Clique' and 'The Rafter Party' with their respective benefits. He continues, detailing the exclusive content available, such as episodes on the business side of fashion, launching successful brands, and designer case studies, as well as early access to audio versions. Visually, the user navigates the 'Threaducation Patreon Page', first to the 'About' section displaying a welcome message with a Virgil Abloh quote, then scrolling to the 'Latest posts' section where two unlocked episodes, 'The History of Prada' and '#1 - How to Develop a Signature Product', are visible and marked as 'JUST NOW'. The narrator further explains that joining is a way to support the channel and enable more full-time free video production. He then transitions to the main content, expressing excitement for the episode, "This is the History of Prada." Concurrently, the user clicks to play 'The History of Prada Episode' on the Patreon page, which then transitions to a title card featuring bold black text on a white background, accompanied by a subtle, atmospheric background music track. The video then cuts to grainy, dimly lit 'Archival Fashion Show Footage', showing a model walking a runway in a venue with an audience, capturing a vintage live fashion event atmosphere, while the atmospheric background music continues and the narrator begins the historical discussion, stating, "To take things back to the very beginning..."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with grainy, dimly lit archival footage of a fashion show, where models in dark, tailored ensembles walk a runway flanked by seated audiences, set against classical architectural elements like a chandelier and columns. Over this vintage scene, a narrator begins to recount the history of Prada, starting in 1857 with the birth of Mario Prada in Milan, Italy. The narrator details Mario's early career as a salesman in a local leather goods shop, where he meticulously learned the leather trade, including tanning processes and distinguishing high-quality products. As Mario refined his skills and knowledge, he decided to launch his own leather goods shop. The visual then transitions to a black-and-white still image of the historic 'Fratelli Prada' storefront in Milan, adorned with ornate detailing and overlaid with the text 'THREADUCATION'. The narrator explains that in 1913, Mario, with his brother Martino, officially opened this shop, 'Fratelli Prada,' which translates to 'The Prada Brothers.' They made, imported, and sold a range of leather goods, including trunks and bags. The video then cuts back to more archival footage, followed by a brightly lit, modern runway featuring a model in a light blue blazer, as the narrator emphasizes Mario's ambition to do things differently, focusing on brand positioning from the outset.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute begins with what is presented as archival fashion show footage, though it features a modern aesthetic: a model confidently strides down a white, minimalist runway in a light blue blazer with a graphic back print, observed by an engaged audience. The setting, with its neutral walls and soft lighting, emphasizes the structured, tailored garments. As a male narrator explains Mario Prada's acute understanding of customer perception and the necessity for high-quality goods to be *perceived* as such, another model in a dark tailored coat enters, followed by a third in a similar dark ensemble, all maintaining a poised demeanor. The narrator continues, stressing that customers wouldn't spend thousands in a "dusty old leather shop," regardless of product quality. The visual then transitions to a still image of the opulent Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II in Milan, a grand shopping arcade with a glass dome and luxury stores like Prada and Savini, featuring a 'THREADUCATION' brand overlay. The narrator explicitly connects this visual, stating Mario strategically opened Fratelli Prada at this very Galleria, which he describes as the oldest shopping mall in Italy and home to major luxury brands, to position Prada's leather goods among the elite. The video then returns to the runway, showcasing a model in a dark coat with a structured collar walking towards the camera, followed by another in a long, patterned coat, continuing the display of sophisticated, minimalist fashion, as the narrator concludes that this strategic approach established Prada as a premier leather shop across Italy.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with archival fashion show footage, showcasing models in dark, tailored outfits walking a runway in a classical setting, as a male narrator explains that in 1919, just six years after its opening, Fratelli Prada was named the official supplier of the Italian Royal Household, providing all their luggage, bags, and other leather goods. This historical context is followed by brightly lit modern runway footage, where models showcase structured, sophisticated looks: a long, geometric patterned coat, a beige coat with a dark skirt, and a vibrant yellow top with a green skirt. The narrator highlights that being named the official supplier gave Prada the right to use the House of Savoy's Coat of Arms on their products, which is why it's often incorporated into the actual Prada logo. This explanation coincides with a brief cut to a graphic comparing the House of Savoy Coat of Arms with the Prada logo, displaying 'THREADUCATION'. The scene then returns to the modern runway, featuring a model in a dark green coat and purple tights, as the narrator continues, noting that the royal family's endorsement solidified Prada's status as an ultra-luxury brand, allowing it to continue to grow. The segment concludes with the narrator mentioning that in 1958, Mario Prada passed away at 101, leaving behind a strong legacy and posing the question of who would continue it and ensure the Prada name was known around the world.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a close-up of a model with short, dark hair, confidently walking a minimalist white runway in a structured dark green coat over a purple garment, her hand poised on her hip. Simultaneously, a male voice narrates the early history of Prada, explaining Mario Prada's patriarchal mindset and his initial reluctance for women in his family to join the business. As the camera follows the model, revealing a formally dressed audience observing intently, the narration continues, detailing how Mario's son had no interest in inheriting the brand. The scene transitions to a blonde model in a teal coat over a purple dress, walking with composed posture, followed by a model with reddish-brown hair showcasing a sleeveless beige and purple color-block dress with light tights and tan platform heels. The voice-over explains that Mario's daughter, Louisa Prada, took on the challenge, helping Prada affirm its status as one of the world's top leather goods companies over the next two decades. A model in a bold, multi-colored geometric-patterned coat with blue, yellow, green, and black blocks, paired with black tights and heeled boots, is then captured from multiple angles, emphasizing the structured silhouette and vibrant colors. The narration concludes by highlighting the pivotal moment in 1978 when Louisa's daughter, Maria Bianchi, decided to join the company. The segment ends with a group of models, led by one in a mustard-yellow jumpsuit with matching platform heels, walking in formation down the brightly lit runway, with some audience members clapping, all while the historical narrative of Prada unfolds.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with grainy, dimly lit archival footage of a vintage-style fashion show, where models confidently walk a runway showcasing structured, geometric-patterned garments in earthy tones and pastels, as a male narrator discusses a "family business." He introduces "Maria Bianchi," who he reveals is now known as "Miuccia Prada," born in 1949 to Luigi Bianchi and Louisa Prada. A title card displaying 'MIUCCIA' with 'THREADUCATION' beneath appears, followed by a close-up of Miuccia Prada herself, a woman with shoulder-length light brown hair in a dark blazer, speaking directly to the camera against a neutral background. The narrator explains that Miuccia, whose family called her Miuccia, had an interesting path into fashion, noting that the Prada family initially focused on leather accessories like bags and luggage, not fashion. The scene cuts back to the runway, featuring models in avant-garde outfits, including a sheer black top with a structured skirt and a floral-patterned green shirt. The narrator continues, explaining Miuccia fell in love with fashion at a young age. The segment concludes with Miuccia Prada being warmly embraced by smiling women in a celebratory setting, then speaking into a microphone with a 'T' logo, likely for 'THREADUCATION', all set to a consistent, upbeat electronic background music.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a static image featuring two individuals dressed as mimes, complete with black-and-white striped shirts, berets, white face paint, and gloves, posing against a white background with the text 'THREADUCATION' at the bottom. Over this visual, a male narrator explains that the subject of the discussion, surprisingly, once wanted to be a mime as a career, spending years studying and performing at the Piccolo Teatro in Milan. The scene then transitions to a close-up of a Woman in Dark Blazer, her light brown hair falling over her shoulders, speaking expressively into a Microphone with a 'T' Logo, smiling and gesturing, set in a dimly lit interview space with a dark green backdrop. The narrator continues, revealing that around the same time, she also pursued political science, eventually earning her PhD from the University of Milan, and was an outspoken member of Italy's Communist Party, a stance he explains was common for those embracing leftist ideologies and feminism. As the narration progresses, the video cuts to grainy, dimly lit archival fashion show footage, first focusing on models’ feet in distinctive colorful boots—first green, then gold—as they walk down a runway. The camera then pans up to show a Model in a Teal Coat over a purple dress walking confidently, followed by a wider shot of the runway with an audience seated under a chandelier, evoking a vintage, elegant atmosphere, all while the narrator concludes his explanation of her political motivations.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with grainy, dimly lit archival fashion show footage, capturing models walking down a narrow runway flanked by dark curtains. A male narrator explains that Prada needed to remain relevant and do things differently, beginning with refining a technique to make bags out of waterproof nylon fabric. The models wear minimalist, structured outfits in neutral tones—light blazers, tailored skirts, and sheer tops—exhibiting a stark, avant-garde aesthetic, as the camera follows their poised, deliberate strides. A close-up reveals a model in a white sleeveless top with a black belt, followed by another in a light blue top with a knotted front. The scene transitions to a black-and-white still image of Patrizio Bertelli and Miuccia Prada, labeled with their names and the 'THREADUCATION' watermark. The narrator details that in 1977, Miuccia attended a trade show for leather goods companies where she met Patrizio Bertelli, who owned a similar business. By 1978, the two were married, and that same year, Luisa Prada decided to retire, making Miuccia the one in charge. With Patrizio's help, Miuccia set out to reinvent the brand, wasting no time. The footage returns to the runway, showing models in various ensembles, including one in a sheer, ruffled top and another in a light-colored, textured dress. The narrator adds that in 1979, Prada introduced its first-ever line of women's footwear, a huge step in the right direction that showed they were capable of making products other than trunks and handbags. The low lighting continues to focus attention on the garments and the models’ confident expressions, as the sequence ends with a model in a sheer, patterned top walking away from the camera, her back to the audience, as the runway fades into darkness, with the narrator concluding that in the mid-1980s, Miuccia finally decided to unveil the nylon bags.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a black screen before transitioning to a static, high-contrast image of a black Prada hobo bag, featuring a silver triangular logo and a single shoulder strap, set against a white background with 'THREADUCATION' text. Throughout this visual, a male narrator explains how Prada, initially an ultra-luxury brand known for high-quality leather goods, controversially introduced nylon fabric into its designs. He highlights that nylon was typically reserved for rain or athletic gear due to its water-resistant, durable, and stretchy properties, making its inclusion in luxury items by Miuccia an unexpected yet brilliant move. The scene then abruptly cuts to grainy, dimly lit archival fashion show footage, where a woman with shoulder-length light brown hair, wearing a dark blazer over a light blue top, is seen speaking expressively into a microphone with a 'T' logo. This is followed by a model walking down a dark, narrow runway in a light-colored, textured dress with a ruffled hem and a bow at the waist, her long, wavy hair flowing. The low-resolution footage, with a visible CBC logo, serves as a backdrop as the narrator continues, revealing that Prada's growth had plateaued due to a lack of innovation, having relied on the same product offerings since 1913, and that Miuccia's introduction of nylon was a strategic response to this stagnation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a model walking down a dimly lit runway in a dark, floral-patterned dress with a high collar and sleek updo, as a male speaker begins to describe a designer's innovative approach: taking "loud prints and bright colors" and working them into "very modern, clean-cut garments," a concept he notes "just hadn't been done." The scene then transitions to a brighter, modern runway with white walls and a seated audience, while the speaker identifies the collection as Prada's Spring Summer 1996, titled "Banal Eccentricity," which he calls the "inception of ugly chic." Subsequent models appear, showcasing a vibrant yellow top with floral pants, a geometric-patterned dress with a structured silhouette, and layered ensembles including a yellow jacket over a green plaid skirt, a long yellow coat over patterned shorts, and a sleeveless green dress. The camera captures close-ups of the bold fabric textures and graphic designs, emphasizing the color blocking and minimalist tailoring. The speaker elaborates that many looks featured patterns that "wouldn't work well together at all" if not for the "complimentary color palettes," or the same pattern repeated in "three different colors." Despite this, he concludes that the collection "comes off as effortlessly stylish," or "chic," quoting Miuccia Prada who reflected, "Ugly is attractive. Ugly is exciting. Maybe because it's newer." A subtle, upbeat electronic music track plays continuously in the background, enhancing the high fashion atmosphere, and the 'FF' logo is visible in the bottom right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with archival fashion show footage, as a male voiceover begins to discuss the impact of a fashion trend, explaining it had "never really been seen before" or "executed this well." A model in a sleeveless green dress with a deep V-neck walks confidently down a white runway, flanked by seated audience members. The camera follows her from behind, then cuts to a second model in a white short-sleeved shirt and brown skirt, walking toward the camera with a composed expression. The voiceover continues, identifying this as the origin of "ugly chic" and a style a designer continued to embrace. The camera zooms in on the second model's face before pulling back, followed by a third model in a brown sleeveless fitted dress, walking with poise and turning at the end of the runway. The voiceover explains that the designer would "deviate from it every now and then" but was "always looking to challenge the status quo," acknowledging that this "does of course open you up to criticism." The scene transitions to a black screen, then to a white title card reading 'THE RED LINE' with 'THREADUCATION' beneath it, while the voiceover explicitly names "Miuccia" (referring to Miuccia Prada), who "climbed her way to the top of the fashion industry" and was "just getting started." A calm, atmospheric instrumental track begins to play as the video cuts to a new fashion show scene with dim lighting, featuring a model in a light-colored, patterned dress with a belted waist, walking down a runway with a serious expression. The camera focuses on her high-heeled shoes with black tips and gold embellishments before the voiceover returns, stating that "Fashion fanatics around the world were quickly falling in love with Prada, but they were also falling in love with Miuccia herself," as the scene ends.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with archival fashion show footage, featuring a male model walking a dimly lit runway in a structured beige coat, white shirt, and trousers, with a brown satchel, followed by a close-up of his brown leather boots. A male narrator explains that the launch of Prada's first menswear collection marked a new chapter for the brand, one influenced by Neil Barrett, who shared Miuccia Prada's affinity for elegant minimalism and high-performance materials like waterproof nylon. The scene transitions to an interview with Miuccia Prada, identified on-screen, smiling and speaking into a microphone in a well-lit showroom, as the narrator details how Neil Barrett used nylon and other industrial-grade materials to craft entire collections, eventually launching the 'Prada Sport' diffusion label in 1997. The runway footage resumes, showcasing models in beige coats, a green polo shirt with grey trousers, and a series of models in minimalist, monochromatic ensembles—white hooded jackets, sleeveless tops, and loose trousers—some with red accents, walking in unison. The narrator describes Prada Sport as a line of high-end, functional, and stylish sportswear, suitable for both workouts and everyday wear, noting it was a continuation of Miuccia's earlier work with nylon. The segment concludes with a model in a striking red hooded cape alongside another in a white high-collared outfit, with audience members observing under soft, diffused lighting, as the narrator emphasizes the importance of branding in a market where other luxury brands were introducing similar products.

segments.integrated_caption: The video begins with grainy, dimly lit archival fashion show footage, where models in minimalist, structured neutral outfits walk a narrow runway. A narrator introduces Prada's "now iconic red line," describing it as an "incredibly simple yet striking" design element that, despite its tiny size, makes any product instantly recognizable as Prada. This commentary accompanies a transition to static product images, including a black Prada puffer jacket featuring the distinctive red 'PRADA' logo on its chest pocket, and a pair of Prada America's Cup sneakers in navy, white, and grey. The narrator explicitly identifies these as the "America's World Cup sneaker" that featured the famous red line on the tongue, explaining they were initially created for "Prada's Luna Rossa sailing team" for the 1997 America's Cup race and became an "instant hit" with the public. This is visually reinforced by a group photo of the team in white shirts standing in front of their Prada-branded 'LUNA ROSSA' racing yacht. The narrator concludes by stating that "products like these helped Prada Sport achieve immense success," but foreshadows that "it wouldn't last forever," as "Neil Barrett left Prada" in 1999 to launch his own label, having "accomplished what he wanted at the fashion house." The text 'THREADUCATION' is consistently displayed beneath each product image. The segment then returns to the archival fashion show footage, showing more models in white ensembles, including one with a white fanny pack and another in a white suit, moving with composed strides as the audience observes.

segments.integrated_caption: The video captures a dynamic fashion show unfolding on a narrow, dimly lit runway, where models showcase a diverse range of avant-garde designs. A male narrator's voice overlays the scene, discussing the business history of fashion, starting with a reference to 'the now classic 2006 film The Devil Wears Prada.' As models confidently stride past intently watching audience members, displaying outfits like a red-haired model in a patterned sleeveless top and orange pleated skirt, or a blonde model in a dark brown polo and shorts with a colorful crossbody bag, the narrator details how annual sales for a major fashion house soared past the $1 billion mark in the 2000s. The camera captures close-ups of intricate details, such as a green jacket over a striped top with a bold orange-striped green skirt, and a model in a brown leather dress, while the narrator explains the challenges of running such a large company and Miuccia's oversight of Miu Miu's expansion into eyewear and fragrances. Further models appear, including one in a dramatic peacock-feather patterned outfit, another with a feathered hat, a vibrant deep red jumpsuit, and a final assertive look featuring a black coat and tall boots. Throughout these visual transitions, the narrator continues, reflecting on how some aspects of the brand, like Prada Sport, lost momentum after a key designer left, leading to a rebranding as Prada Linea Rossa (Italian for 'the red line'), which eventually faded from the spotlight before being brought back in 2018.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dynamic fashion show, featuring models confidently walking a narrow, dimly lit runway with a textured metallic surface, flanked by seated audience members holding phones and cameras. As a model in a black outfit with a red leg stripe and black boots passes, followed by another in a white oversized coat, white shorts, and a white bucket hat, accessorized with a red 'PRADA' tag, a male narrator discusses the 'Linea Rossa' line, noting its incorporation into Prada's Fall/Winter 2018 collections. The narrator explains that Prada confirmed its return as a 'new facet in the Prada universe,' which is still 'going strong today,' with many popular products stemming from it. A subsequent model in a grey coat with a similar red 'PRADA' tag and grey bucket hat reinforces the visual theme. The scene then transitions to a still image of Miuccia Prada in a green satin blouse and Raf Simons in a white shirt with an 'RS' monogram, under the text 'MIUCCIA PRADA AND RAF SIMONS' and 'THREADUCATION'. The narrator introduces this as a 'new facet' from February 2020, announcing that 'the legendary designer Raf Simmons would be joining Miuccia as the co-creative director of Prada.' The announcement, which 'seemed to have come out of nowhere,' signified that 'the two of them were going to be doing all of it together,' not just separate men's and women's wear. This is visually underscored by Raf Simons, wearing a black turtleneck with a red collar, speaking expressively with hand gestures in a professional interview setting with a modern interior.

The overall atmosphere of the discussion scene is calm and professional, highlighting the speakers and the Prada branding, while the narrator provides historical and contemporary context for the brand.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Raf Simons, seated on a minimalist white bench in a textured grey sweater, listening intently with his hands clasped. A male narrator's voice begins, providing a historical overview of Prada, explaining how "Louisa passed the business onto her own daughter, Miuccia," and how "the trajectory of Prada changed forever" under her guidance. The camera then cuts to a wide shot, revealing Miuccia Prada, dressed in a dark navy sweater and flowing white skirt, seated opposite Raf on a similar bench. A large screen behind them displays the Prada logo and a question from Vlad Vlasyuk, Kyiv: 'HOW LONG HAVE YOU WANTED TO WORK TOGETHER?'. The narrator continues, detailing how "Prada became a full-fledged fashion house" with Miuccia and her husband Patrizio's help, transforming it into a "global business." Miuccia is seen responding with expressive hand gestures, her demeanor thoughtful and engaged, while Raf maintains a focused expression. The narrator highlights the "reintroduction of Linea Rossa and the appointment of Raf Simons as co-creative director," emphasizing Prada's active investment in its future. The screen changes to a new question from Derek, New York, asking about the ease or difficulty of designing a collection with another designer. Miuccia smiles warmly, and Raf listens reflectively, his hands still clasped, as the narrator concludes by stating that "Miuccia, Patrizio, and Raf are all well aware of the fact that fashion is changing," all while the two designers are visually engaged in a calm, intellectual interaction within the minimalist, high-fashion setting.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of Raf Simons, seated on a minimalist white bench, wearing a textured grey sweater over a black shirt and dark trousers, his hands clasped as he listens intently. The camera then cuts to a wide shot revealing Miuccia Prada seated on a similar bench opposite him, dressed in a dark navy sweater over a white top and a flowing white skirt, with a glass of water beside her. A large screen behind them displays the Prada logo and the question: 'HOW LONG HAVE YOU WANTED TO WORK TOGETHER?' attributed to Vlad Vlasyuk, Kyiv. Miuccia responds with expressive hand gestures, her demeanor thoughtful and engaged. The camera alternates between close-ups of each speaker and wide shots of the stage, emphasizing their dialogue. The screen then changes to a new question: 'SO, WAS IT MUCH EASIER TO DESIGN A COLLECTION WITH ANOTHER DESIGNER? OR WAS IT MUCH HARDER? (I’M SURE IT WAS MUCH OF ONE OF THEM.)' from Derek, New York. Miuccia smiles warmly, and Raf listens with a focused, reflective expression, his hands still clasped. The interaction is calm, intellectual, and deeply personal, highlighting the collaborative relationship between the two designers within a minimalist, high-fashion context.
